title: A Totally Fun Thing That Bart Will Never

jokes.text: Sorry, kid, but there comes a day when that just doesn't do anything anymore.
jokes.setup: Child presumably trying to use an arcade machine or game
jokes.punchline: Blunt existential observation about childhood joy expiring
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about lost innocence delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Sets tone for episode's themes.

jokes.text: We built heaven and made it float.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously grandiose claim about building heaven
jokes.explanation: Solid parody of cruise marketing's over-the-top promises

jokes.text: Want, want, want, want, want, want, want, want, want! Want!!
jokes.punchline: Accelerating repetition building to desperate scream
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart characterization - impulsive desire spiraling out of control

jokes.text: do you know there's a boat out there where nothing sucks?
jokes.punchline: Child's simple worldview: boat where 'nothing sucks'
jokes.explanation: Bart's innocent yet profane summary of paradise is endearing and funny

jokes.text: Maybe we could afford a vacation if some big shot didn't pick up the tab for his buddies at Mr. Steak.
jokes.punchline: Marge's passive-aggressive dig at Homer's spending
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge-Homer dynamic with Mr. Steak as recurring financial drain

jokes.text: Because at Mr. Steak, they don't get mad at me for ending a lot of money at Mr. Steak!
jokes.setup: Homer defending his Mr. Steak spending
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that restaurants don't mind when you spend money there
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - completely missing the point while being technically correct

jokes.text: There is no Mrs. Steak! Who could stay married to such a man?
jokes.setup: Homer's defense of Mr. Steak spending
jokes.punchline: Marge's dramatic condemnation of fictional Mr. Steak
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Marge creates entire tragic backstory for restaurant owner

jokes.text: Face it, we're just kids. We can't afford stuff with zeroes in the prices.
jokes.punchline: Child's perspective on expensive items having 'zeroes'
jokes.explanation: Clever child's view of expensive things - anything with 'zeroes' is unattainable

jokes.text: Bart! There's a really mean squirrel in the backyard. I need you to dare me to fight him!
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous request to be dared to fight a squirrel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse - needs external validation for absurdly minor act of bravery

jokes.text: Bart's been raptured! And his crap's been craptured!
jokes.punchline: Rhyming rapture with 'craptured' for possessions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'craptured' is both linguistically clever and perfectly childish

jokes.text: Doctor says I need more calcium, and I ain't 'Tums Rich.'
jokes.punchline: Gil's poverty expressed through antacid wealth metaphor
jokes.explanation: 'Tums Rich' is a perfect Gil-ism - turning antacid affordability into wealth status

jokes.text: I can't, I sold my 'dinner futures.'
jokes.setup: Marge telling Bart to enjoy dinner
jokes.punchline: Treating dinner like a commodity to be traded
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economic absurdism - applying futures trading to basic meals

jokes.text: I'm going to floor.
jokes.punchline: Can't even afford to say 'the floor' - just 'floor'
jokes.explanation: Subtle poverty humor - can't afford articles like 'the'

jokes.text: I woke up and the money jar was full! That means the devil accepted my bargain.
jokes.punchline: Assuming he made a deal with Satan
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - child casually assuming supernatural intervention

jokes.text: Now, to uphold my end of the deal! Snowball II! No!
jokes.punchline: Apparently sacrificing the family cat
jokes.explanation: Escalates the devil joke to logical dark conclusion

jokes.text: After a while they all start to sound the same. Still love the genre, of course. Not even close to getting sick of it.
jokes.punchline: Obvious defensiveness about getting tired of jazz
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa - trying to maintain intellectual credibility while admitting jazz fatigue

jokes.text: actually, my mom stole it from a woman she cleaned for. Took her years to get the whole set.
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing it was stolen over years
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - family treasure becomes multi-year theft operation

jokes.text: I never even got to realize this jacket was too small.
jokes.punchline: Missed opportunity to outgrow clothing
jokes.explanation: Subtle sadness about missing normal childhood experiences

jokes.text: For dads, a family vacation is a 24-hour-a-day babysitting job.
jokes.punchline: Vacation as extended childcare duty
jokes.explanation: Relatable dad complaint but fairly predictable observation

jokes.text: Then a kid disappears, and it's total panic until you find him at the swim-up bar... eating a 30-dollar cheeseburger, which he drops in the water!
jokes.punchline: Specific escalating scenario ending in expensive food waste
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation with specific, believable details

jokes.text: Welcome to eight days and seven nights of pushups and kale!
jokes.punchline: Sugar-free fitness cruise sounds like torture
jokes.explanation: Effective contrast between dream cruise and nightmare alternative

jokes.text: Well, well, Mr. Never-Wrong was right again: Family vacations always suck. I called it! Called... it.
jokes.punchline: Homer's smug vindication deflating as he realizes consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - being right but realizing victory is hollow

jokes.text: ♪ Worth it to be ri-ght! ♪
jokes.punchline: Still trying to celebrate being right despite circumstances
jokes.explanation: Homer's stubborn commitment to being vindicated even when it hurts him

jokes.text: Double upgrade! All right!
jokes.punchline: Homer's misguided enthusiasm for each setback
jokes.explanation: Homer's optimistic misinterpretation of being shuffled around

jokes.text: I knew it. It was all a dream.
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical assumption it's too good to be true
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - sets up false 'it was all a dream' before revealing truth

jokes.text: No, honey. You just passed out when you saw the private bedroom in our triple upgrade!
jokes.punchline: Even better reality - Bart literally fainted from luxury
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to misdirection - reality is better than dream

jokes.text: Your enjoyment pays my salary, so in a sense, each of you is my boss... and as my boss... I'd like to ask you for a week of vacation.
jokes.setup: Cruise director explaining customer service philosophy
jokes.punchline: Using logic to request vacation from passengers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical loop - customers as bosses leads to vacation request

jokes.text: Ocean sex rules! Go to hell, land sex!
jokes.punchline: Declaring war on terrestrial intimacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer escalation - turning personal discovery into cosmic declaration

jokes.text: Let me guess: a broken foosball table and a game of Clue missing its candlestick.
jokes.punchline: Cynical prediction of cheap entertainment
jokes.explanation: Specific, relatable prediction of disappointing kids' areas

jokes.text: I've died and gone to a PBS Kids show!
jokes.punchline: Perfect environment described as educational TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa reaction - paradise as educational programming

jokes.text: Two diplomats' sons are in love with me.
jokes.punchline: Even romance is internationally sophisticated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa fantasy - even her love triangle is diplomatic

jokes.text: I don't know, a lot of barefoot kids kicking soccer balls, shell necklaces; they really hid the poverty nicely.
jokes.punchline: Casually noting sanitized presentation of third-world conditions
jokes.explanation: Dark observation about tourist industry sanitizing poverty

jokes.text: Your names...
jokes.punchline: Marge's embarrassed reaction to oversharing
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge embarrassment at Homer's public crudeness

jokes.text: Here at sea, we drink and frolic / Back home, that's called alcoholic
jokes.punchline: Vacation drinking vs. problem drinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant rhyme highlighting vacation/reality double standard

jokes.text: After this cruise, for the rest of my life, it's all downhill.
jokes.punchline: 10-year-old having existential crisis about peak experience
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - child having midlife crisis about having peaked

jokes.text: Haw-haw!
jokes.punchline: Nelson's signature laugh at emotional vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson timing - laughing at existential dread

jokes.text: 'Chillingly plausible' indeed, Joe Morgenstern of The Wall Street Journal.
jokes.punchline: Quoting movie critic review of his deception source
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about movie reviews validating his scheme's credibility

jokes.text: While the world deals with their pandemic, we're having a 'fundemic,'
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'fun' and 'pandemic'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay maintaining upbeat cruise director persona

jokes.text: I always knew mankind's hubris would doom us all.
jokes.punchline: Fatalistic acceptance of human nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scandinavian fatalism about human civilization

jokes.text: Hey, so what's the deal with how many Starbucks there used to be, huh? I don't know why that bothered me so much. Hug your kids.
jokes.punchline: Trivial complaint becomes profound in context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - standup comedy tropes in apocalyptic context

jokes.text: Mankind was warned not to run near the pool! Mankind did not listen! The virus is his reward!
jokes.punchline: Pool safety rules as cosmic punishment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of lifeguard authority to religious prophecy

jokes.text: Drop dead, fat-ass! Last week, that steward brought me extra shampoo. He called me 'lovely lady.'
jokes.punchline: Nostalgic for recent good service while being insulted
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between current hostility and recent kindness

jokes.text: Erik, we must work with the humans. You are so naive, Charles. We should rule them as conquerors.
jokes.punchline: Treating adults as different species to be ruled
jokes.explanation: Brilliant child perspective - adults as separate species

jokes.text: General, it's worse than we thought. The virus is getting bigger.
jokes.punchline: Treating virus like a physical growing entity
jokes.explanation: B-movie virus logic - viruses getting physically larger

jokes.text: A direct-to-video DVD.
jokes.punchline: Adding insult - not even theatrical release
jokes.explanation: Extra humiliation - fooled by bottom-tier movie quality

jokes.text: Your fun-ishment: eight hours in the penal conga line.
jokes.setup: Cruise director maintaining fun theme despite crisis
jokes.punchline: Punishment as 'fun-ishment' - forced fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect cruise director commitment to forced fun even as punishment

jokes.text: You know, I thought that general looked familiar. What? I'm in a lot of movies.
jokes.punchline: Actor defending his prolific B-movie career
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about actor's career being recognizable from quantity

jokes.text: I think I ate people meat!
jokes.punchline: Casual cannibalism concern
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy escalation of cruise food quality decline

jokes.text: After this, I'll be lucky to get work on a clothing-optional Jimmy Buffett cruise!
jokes.punchline: Specific degrading cruise assignment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific degradation - combining nudity and Buffett music as career low

jokes.text: Choke on my numb blue hands!
jokes.punchline: Antarctic conditions affecting insults
jokes.explanation: Environment-specific insult - even threats affected by extreme cold

jokes.text: They spend all their time trying to raise a baby, and why? So it can grow up and do the same dumb thing, on and on forever.
jokes.punchline: Existential observation about life cycles
jokes.explanation: Profound nihilistic observation from 10-year-old about life's purpose

jokes.text: But the slide's just like our cruise: a short awesome thing that just shows you how boring the rest of your life is.
jokes.punchline: Metaphor about brief pleasure highlighting life's monotony
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor tying penguin behavior to episode's central theme - exceptional craft

jokes.text: Yeah, stupid. Stop thinking about fun and have it!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's uncharacteristic anti-intellectual advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect role reversal - intellectual Lisa advocating mindless enjoyment

tmdb_overview: When Bart gets his wish for a family vacation, he becomes determined to make it last forever.
